focal_character.name: Yejide Adeyemi
focal_character.occupation: fabric quality inspector at a garment factory
focal_character.ordinary_life_state: Yejide has moved into a supervisory cubicle at the garment factory with her name on the glass. When a production line stalls, the floor supervisor brings the problem to her, and she identifies the missing cotton shipment that everyone else overlooked while cataloguing visible data.

problem_structure.unknown: the missing indigo sample — supplier Oke-Aro, lot 41 — that should hang on the empty hook between the blue-tagged and white-tagged hooks
data: bolts of fabric on pallets — ankara, wax print, plain cotton
brass hooks with sample tags arranged by colour, one hook empty between blue and white
a delivery manifest on the inspection table showing an indigo sample that should be present but has no tag on the hooks
problem_structure.condition: closure responds to the absence being named, not to the present items being arranged, matched, or catalogued

gate_details.correct_approach: Stand at the threshold, look at the whole room, find the empty hook, check the manifest for what should be there, and name the missing item. The gate responds to naming the absence, not to arranging what is present.

new_texture: A garment factory inspection floor with a mezzanine, cubicles named in glass, and metal trays of fabric swatches
Production lines identified by colour runs; bolts come out with shading bands when the wrong base cotton is used during an indigo run
Floor supervisors dial mills directly when a missing shipment stalls a production line

anchor_update.observation:
Gate G-006 (Grade 2)
Entrants: four.
Approach A (executive pattern-match) — Register 1 throughout. Room sorts by colour; hook 4 remains empty, not receding.
Approach B (grid taxonomy) — Register 1 with false 3 flicker on completion. Room mirrors categorical grid; hook 4 sits in cell ‘unmatched’.
Approach C (full audit) — Register 1 undisturbed. Room yields complete catalogue; item 4 listed as ‘empty’, no query.
Timeout at 14:02:17 — no closure.
Approach D (threshold-standing, gaze-to-gaps) — Register 2 at sit. Hand on hook 4 at 14:03:51. Latch at 14:04:03. Gate closed 14:04:05.
— Gap: absence unnamed by A, B, C. Named by D.
anchor_update.reader_can_suspect_update: The anchor now logs register transitions with sub-second precision and notes the exact moment the absence is named. Their notation confirms that 'what is missing' is the pivot — the solvers who catalogued, matched, or audited never asked that question, and the gate remained inert until it was asked.

macro_mystery_update.evidence: A torn notebook page found inside the Lagos fabric-inspection gate records all four solver approaches with register timestamps at second-level precision, showing the anchor can observe multiple solvers within the same gate event and diagnose their mental frames in real time across different approaches.
